messages.content:
Write a 2 paragraph vignette about how i met my best friend in preschool and how we are still friends and do everything together. His name is Adriano. Follow these guildlines : The House on Mango Street Vignette CPT 
The House on Mango Street is organized into a collection of short memories, called vignettes.  
Throughout these vignettes, Esperanza is trying to come to terms with her identity. For this  assignment, I want you to consider the following: Who are you?  
After you have read The House on Mango Street, follow the directions provided below to create your  own collection of vignettes.  
Your steps: 

1. Read The House on Mango Street to see how Sandra Cisneros creates a collection of  vignettes that tell a story.  
2. Read How Do I Write a Vignette? to become more familiar with the characteristics of  a vignette.  
3. Complete the Theme Brainstorming to get you started with some writing ideas 
4. Start drafting!  
5. Revise for clarity and impact.  
Assignment Requirements:  
∙ Create a cover page for your “book” of vignettes. This cover page should include the  title of your book, your name, and  relevant illustrations that go with the themes of your vignettes
∙ You must have two typed vignettes that are at least two paragraphs in length.  These paragraphs should be substantial. However, none of your vignettes should be  longer than two pages.  
∙ Each vignette must convey a theme (think of the ones we have discussed in class) and  each vignette must focus on a different theme (in other words, don’t write four separate  vignettes about friendship).  
∙ Each vignette should have a title.  
∙ Each new vignette should start on a new page.  
∙ Each vignette must have at least two examples of figurative language (simile,  metaphor, personification for example—look these up if you cannot remember what they  are).  
∙ Each vignette must have description (imagery) to help your reader feel immersed in  the story.  
-Include a visual for each vignette that captures the essence or theme of your vignette.
∙ This is your time to break away from the traditional essay format. You can choose  whichever font you like; however, it must be easy to read. Use 11 or 12 point font please.  Be creative and have fun. ☺ 
How Do I Write a Vignette? 
Remember that a vignette is: 
● A form of a personal narrative or story (you should use personal pronouns in this form of  writing)  
● A short, descriptive passage  
● a memory, thought, hope, dream, or idea  
A vignette often includes:  
∙ Setting—background  
∙ Characters—may be real or fictional  
∙ Imagery & Figurative Language—provide vivid and descriptive details to help readers visualize  the story  
∙ Theme—the insight about life  
Think of a vignette as a short story, focused on a specific time, event, or moment in your life. It  captures one moment, just as a photo does. You will tell this story using DETAIL,  DESCRIPTION, & IMAGERY. Dive into the story like Esperanza does--make the reader feel  like he/she is there with you. 
Here are some ideas to get you started. You do not have to use these: 
- What is one of your favorite memories?  
- What is your family like? Similarities? Differences?  
- Have you moved to a new place? Describe that move or your experience.  -Describe a best friend or person close to you.  
- What is a childhood memory you have?  
- Describe an elementary  school experience.  
- Describe an experience where you’ve grown as a person.  
- Describe a family tradition or holiday experience

Title:  Adriano, My Best Friend 

I met Adriano on the first day of preschool. My mom was nervous about leaving me there, but I was excited to start my school journey. As soon as I walked into the classroom, I saw Adriano playing with building blocks alone, and I immediately went over to join him. He looked up and smiled with the brightest grin I had ever seen. We started building tall towers together, and I knew that we would be great friends. 

Now, years later, Adriano is still my best friend. We do everything together, whether it's studying for exams or exploring new neighborhoods in our city. We have experienced so many things together, like learning how to ride bikes or watching our favorite movies. One of our favorite things to do is to try new foods, and we have become foodies over the years. I'm grateful to have met Adriano in preschool because our friendship has truly stood the test of time, and I know that he will always be there for me. 

Title:  The Power of Friendship 

Friendship has always been important to me, and I never realized just how much until I met Adriano. I always knew that being with friends made me happy, but Adriano showed me the true power of friendship. He was there for me during some of my toughest moments and always knew how to make me smile. We have grown together, pushing each other to be better and trying new experiences together. 

Through our friendship, I have learned that friends can be the family you choose. They can be your support system and your confidante. Adriano and I have seen each other through countless ups and downs, but our friendship has never wavered. I have come to appreciate the value of true friendship, and I know that I will always cherish the memories and experiences I have shared with Adriano.
Write the first vignette inot 4 paragraphs add how he always come over to my house

Title: The Best Thing Since Sliced Bread 

There was a time when all I wanted was an imaginary friend. I would think about it in class, on the playground, and even in bed. I needed someone to talk to about everything when no one else was around. That was until I met my neighbor, Adriano. 

Adriano was one year older than me, but we hit it off right away. He lived a block away, and I still remember the first time he came over to my house. We played video games together, and before I knew it, we were talking about everything from our favorite foods to our school crushes. From that day on, it became a routine for Adriano to come over to my house after school. 

My mom would always welcome him with open arms and offer him a snack or drink. She always enjoyed his company as much as I did. We would sit around the kitchen table and talk about our day, and my mom would listen intently and sometimes join in on the conversation. We had become like a little family, and I remember feeling grateful for those moments in my life. 

Adriano became the brother I never had. He was there for me when I was feeling down, and he knew exactly how to make me laugh. It's been many years since we first met, but he still comes over to my house. We play games, watch movies, and reminisce about old times.  It's never boring with Adriano around, and I'm still thankful for the day he walked into my life.
hes not my neighbor

I apologize for the misunderstanding. Here is the revised vignette:

Title: The Best Thing Since Sliced Bread

There was a time when all I wanted was an imaginary friend. I would daydream about it, but in reality, I was shy and had a hard time making friends. That was until I met Adriano in preschool.

Adriano wasn't from my neighborhood, but we had been in the same class since we were four. We bonded over our love for playing superheroes during recess. We would come up with our own characters and catchphrases, battling imaginary monsters. After school, Adriano would always come over to my house to continue our superhero adventures. 

We spent countless afternoons living out our imaginations, jumping on furniture and pretending to fly. My mom would watch us play and make us snacks. She always enjoyed Adriano's company and loved how happy we were together. 

As we got older, our adventures evolved. We would watch movies and play video games together, but we still kept our imaginative spirits alive. Adriano was so much more than just a friend, he was family. He still comes over to my house to this day, and I am continually grateful for his presence in my life.

Adriano is the best thing since sliced bread because he has allowed me to cherish moments that I will always remember with a smile. Our friendship shows that true friendship can keep you together, even when talking about silly things.
change the parts about the imaginary friend

Title: The Adventure Begins

I walked into my preschool classroom not knowing anyone, feeling anxious and alone. Looking around, I saw a boy named Adriano sitting by himself playing with building blocks. I had an urge to go over and join him, and it turned out to be one of the best decisions of my life. 

Adriano and I quickly became inseparable. We were both curious and imaginative, coming up with the wildest ideas during recess. We would play games like "explorers" where we imagined ourselves discovering new lands and making incredible discoveries. After school, Adriano would always come over to my house, and we would continue our adventures. 

Our creativity knew no bounds. We would draw and write our own comic books, create blueprints for our dream treehouses, and even construct our own board games. Our after-school playdates fueled our creative energy, and we were always excited to be together. 

My parents loved having Adriano over. My mom would let us make pizzas together, and my dad would teach us how to play basketball. Our families eventually became like one big family, celebrating holidays and vacations together.

Now years later, Adriano and I are still the best of friends. We have gone through so much together, and I know that I can always rely on him. Our love for creativity and adventure has never faded, and we have even gone on backpacking trips together. The beginning of our friendship marked the start of a never-ending adventure full of laughter and memorable moments.
write as a grade nine student

Title: The Start of an Unlikely Friendship 

Walking into my first day of high school, I was nervous about meeting new people. Little did I know that I was going to meet someone who would turn into my closest friend. 

In my science class, I sat by a guy named Adriano. He was quiet and kept to himself, and I remember thinking that we wouldn't have much in common. But as we started working on group projects together, I realized that he was surprisingly funny and had a witty sense of humor. We eventually exchanged numbers and started hanging out outside of school. 

Adriano and I found ourselves going on many adventures around town, from going to the movies to trying new food joints. What I liked about him was that he was open to trying new things, and our friendship allowed us to have fun and explore our city at the same time. 

Our friendship had a lot of ups and downs. Like any friendship, we had our moments of disagreements, but those moments helped us grow stronger. We talked things out and figured out ways to improve our relationship. In the end, we managed to keep our friendship going, even during the pandemic when we had to social distance from each other. 

It's funny how things worked out. I never thought that I would meet someone like Adriano. He was my classmate, but our friendship ended up being much more than that. It just goes to show that relationships can develop in unexpected places.
write it as a grade nine student but keep the part where we meet the first day of elemetary school

Title: Elementary School Best Friends 

The first day of elementary school was a scary experience for me. I didn't know anyone in my class, and I felt like an outsider. All of that changed when I met Adriano. 

Adriano and I were assigned to sit next to each other in our classroom. We started talking, and I found out that we had a lot in common. We both loved to play video games and read comic books. We bonded over our favorite superheroes and talked about our interests in science and exploring nature. From that day on, we became the best of friends. 

Adriano and I would do everything together. We would have sleepovers almost every weekend, watch our favorite movies and shows, and even start our own science experiments. We were inseparable and had a strong bond. 

As we grew older, our friendship evolved. We went to different high schools, but we still kept in touch. We would text or call each other almost every day and hang out when we had the chance. We would explore the city, visit new restaurants, and try new hobbies. 

What I value most about our friendship is that even though we have different paths ahead of us, we still have each other. Adriano has always been there for me to give me advice, support, and encouragement. I know that we will always remain best friends, no matter what life brings our way.
we go to the same high school

Title: A High School Friendship

Going into high school, I didn't know what to expect. I was scared about meeting new people and finding new friends. That all changed on the first day when I saw a familiar face - Adriano. 

Adriano and I had gone to the same elementary and middle school, and we were excited to be reunited in high school. We had always been close, but our friendship grew even stronger as we navigated high school together. 

Over the years, Adriano and I have been through everything together. We've celebrated each other's birthdays, cheered each other on at sports games, and helped each other through tough times. We've vented to each other about everything from annoying teachers to crushes, and we've always been there to support each other. 

What makes our friendship special is that we are always there for each other, even when things get tough. High school can be a stressful and overwhelming time, but with Adriano by my side, I know that I can handle anything. We've learned a lot about each other and ourselves, and we've grown together. 

I'm so grateful for Adriano and all that he brings to our friendship. He makes me laugh, he supports me, and he challenges me to be my best self. I know that our friendship will only continue to grow stronger as we move through high school and beyond.
we met in elementary school

Title: From Elementary School Buddies to Lifelong Friends 

When I first started elementary school, I was nervous about making new friends. That all changed when I met Adriano. We were in the same grade and quickly became friends. 

Adriano and I bonded over our love of sports, comics, and video games. We would spend hours on the playground, competing against each other in games of basketball or soccer. After school, we would play video games together and debate which superheroes were the best. 

As we got older, our interests changed, but our friendship stayed strong. We would talk about our dreams for the future, our favorite classes, and the things that scared us. We would have sleepovers, where we would stay up late, talking and laughing. 

Now that we're in high school, I'm grateful that our friendship has withstood the test of time. Although we don't see each other as often as we used to, we still catch up and make time to hang out. Our conversations have become more mature, but our bond is still the same. We support each other through tough times, make each other laugh when we need it the most, and challenge each other to be better people. 

Adriano is more than just a friend, he's practically family. I'm lucky to have met him in elementary school, and I know that our friendship will continue to grow as we move through high school and beyond.
The most recent vignette is perfect write it again with 6 paragraphs

Title: From Elementary School Buddies to Lifelong Friends 

When I first started elementary school, I was nervous about making new friends. I had just moved to a new city and didn't know anyone in the area. That all changed when I met Adriano. We were in the same grade and sat next to each other in class. Adriano and I quickly became friends. 

Adriano and I bonded over our love of sports, comics, and video games. We would spend hours on the playground, competing against each other in games of basketball or soccer. He had an infectious personality and an ability to make friends easily, so he helped me make new friends too.

After school, we would play video games together and debate which superheroes were the best. We spent almost every day together, either playing sports or hanging out. It wasn't long before our friendship developed from elementary school buddies to lifelong friends.

As we got older, our interests changed, but our friendship stayed strong. We would talk about our dreams for the future, our favorite classes, and the things that scared us. We would have sleepovers, where we would stay up late, talking and laughing. Even in high school, when we started to drift away from some of our old friends, our bond remained unbreakable.

Now that we're in high school, I'm grateful that our friendship has withstood the test of time. Although we don't see each other as often as we used to due to our different schedules, we still catch up and make time to hang out. We've been through a lot together, supporting each other through tough times, but also celebrating each other's victories.

Adriano is more than just a friend. He's like a brother who cares for my well-being and has always been there for me. We've seen each other grow and change, but our friendship has remained the same. We challenge each other to be better people, and we always know how to make each other smile.

I can't imagine going through my school years without Adriano by my side, and I'm lucky to have met him in elementary school. I know that our friendship will only continue to grow as we move through high school and beyond.
Write another vignette similar to the last one but make this one about growing up. ( im 14)

Title: Growing Up with My Best Friend 

Growing up is never easy. It seems like there's always something new to worry about, like new friends, increased responsibilities, and changes in family dynamics. But having a best friend by my side like Adriano has made it easier to navigate the ups and downs of growing up. 

Adriano and I have been best friends for as long as I can remember. When we were younger, we would spend our days running around the playground and playing video games. But as we've grown up, our friendship has changed. Now, we talk about college plans, career aspirations, and political issues.
